Oklahoma Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
Compact Enacted - Not Yet Active

Reciprocity in Oklahoma for Dietitians

Oklahoma has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. Until implementation goes live, apply by endorsement through the state board.

Once active, compact participation should reduce or eliminate the need for a separate transfer application.

Endorsement fee
$120
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete between 1,000 and 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice or an accredited dietetic internship.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

  3. Submit the Online Application

    Apply through the Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure online portal and pay the $120 application fee.

  4. Complete Background Check & Provide Documents

    All applicants must submit to an Extended Background Check. You must also submit primary source verification of your education and passing CDR exam score.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$120
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
$100
Renewal cycle
Annual (by Oct 31st)

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Verification: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration sent directly to the Board.
  • Official College Transcripts: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
  • Extended Background Check: Processed through the Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation (OSBI).
  • Evidence of Legal Status: Form documenting your citizenship or qualified alien status.

Board Contact

Board
Oklahoma Licensed Dietitians Committee
Department
Oklahoma State Board of Medical Licensure and Supervision
Phone
(405) 962-1400
Email
licensing@okmedicalboard.org

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $100. Renewal cycle: Annual (by Oct 31st).

Oklahoma Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does an Oklahoma dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $120. Additional fees apply for the required background check.

When does the Oklahoma license need to be renewed?

The Oklahoma licensed dietitian credential expires annually on October 31st. The renewal fee is $100.

Does Oklahoma license Nutritionists?

No, Oklahoma only issues licenses for Dietitians (Licensed Dietitian). There are no specific licensing requirements or title protections for individuals claiming to be 'nutritionists' in the state.

Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for dietitians?

Oklahoma has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Oklahoma?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
